topic_title: Journey of the Holy Prophet Muhammad (pbuh)
question: The first revelation was received by the Prophet Muhammad (pbuh) in the Cave of _______.

topic_title: Journey of the Holy Prophet Muhammad (pbuh)
question: Describe the event of the first revelation received by the Prophet Muhammad (pbuh). [4 marks]
model_answer: The Prophet (pbuh) was meditating in the Cave of Hira when Angel Jibrail appeared and commanded him to "Iqra" (Read/Recite). The Prophet replied he could not read. The Angel embraced him three times. Then revealed the first five verses of Surah Al-Alaq. The Prophet returned home trembling and was comforted by his wife Khadija (RA) who took him to Warqa bin Naufil.

topic_title: Journey of the Holy Prophet Muhammad (pbuh)
question: Explain what happened during the Isra and Mi'raj (Night Journey and Ascension). [4 marks]
english_translation: The Night Journey and Heavenly Ascension
model_answer: The Isra was the miraculous night journey from Makkah to Jerusalem (Al-Masjid Al-Aqsa) on the Buraq. The Mi'raj was the ascension through the heavens where the Prophet met previous prophets. Allah prescribed 50 prayers, reduced to 5 through Musa's (AS) advice. This event demonstrated the Prophet's special status and established the 5 daily prayers.

topic_title: Journey of the Holy Prophet Muhammad (pbuh)
question: Describe the key events from the first revelation to the Hijrah to Madinah. [8 marks]
model_answer: Level 1 (1-2): Basic mention of the Prophet receiving revelation. Level 2 (3-4): Description of first revelation, early converts (Khadija, Ali, Abu Bakr, Zaid), beginning of public preaching. Level 3 (5-6): Addition of persecution in Makkah, migration to Abyssinia, Year of Sorrow, Ta'if mission, Pledges of Aqabah. Level 4 (7-8): Comprehensive account including specific names, events, causes and significance of each stage. Excellent responses include the Hijrah itself, arrival in Madinah, and the historical significance.

topic_title: The Quran: From Revelation to Compilation
question: The Quran was compiled in book form (Mushaf) during the caliphate of Hazrat _______ (RA).

topic_title: The Quran: From Revelation to Compilation
question: Explain the role of Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA) in the compilation of the Quran. [4 marks]
model_answer: After many Huffaz (memorisers) died in the Battle of Yamama, Hazrat Umar (RA) advised Abu Bakr (RA) to compile the Quran. He appointed Zayd ibn Thabit (RA) to lead the task. Verses were accepted only if confirmed by two witnesses (written and memorised). The result was a single authoritative Mushaf preserved by Abu Bakr (RA) and later passed to Hafsa (RA).

topic_title: The Quran: From Revelation to Compilation
question: Why was the standardisation of the Quranic text under Hazrat Uthman (RA) important? [4 marks]
model_answer: Different regions were using different dialect copies leading to disputes. Hazrat Uthman (RA) formed a committee led by Zayd ibn Thabit to produce a standard text in the Qurayshi dialect. Multiple copies were sent to all provinces. Other copies were burned to prevent division. This preserved unity of the Ummah and ensured the Quran's authentic preservation.

question: The belief in the Oneness of Allah is known as _______ in Arabic.
List and briefly explain the six articles of faith in Islam. [6 marks]
model_answer: 1. Tawheed — Belief in the Oneness of Allah. 2. Malaika — Belief in Angels created from light. 3. Kutub — Belief in the Revealed Books (Torah, Psalms, Gospel, Quran). 4. Rusul — Belief in the Prophets from Adam to Muhammad (pbuh). 5. Yawm al-Qiyamah — Belief in the Day of Resurrection and Judgement. 6. Qadar — Belief in Divine Decree, that Allah has knowledge of all things.

question: The Prophet Muhammad (pbuh) received the first revelation in the Cave of _______.
The Prophet was _______ years old when he received the first revelation.
The first word revealed to the Prophet by Angel Jibreel was '_______'.
After the first revelation, Khadijah (RA) took the Prophet to her cousin _______ ibn Nawfal for guidance.
The first verses revealed belonged to Surah _______.
Describe the circumstances of the first revelation received by the Prophet (pbuh). [4 marks]
model_answer: In 610 CE the Prophet (pbuh) was meditating in Cave Hira during Ramadan. Angel Jibreel appeared and commanded 'Iqra!' three times; each time the Prophet replied he could not read. On the third time the first five verses of Surah Al-Alaq were revealed. The Prophet returned home trembling; Khadijah (RA) comforted him and took him to Waraqah ibn Nawfal who confirmed it was prophethood. This marked the beginning of the 23-year revelation of the Quran.

question: For the first _______ years after the first revelation, the Prophet preached Islam secretly.
The secret meeting place of early Muslims in Makkah was the house of _______ ibn Abi al-Arqam.
The Prophet gathered the Quraysh on Mount _______ to publicly proclaim Islam.
The first freed slave to accept Islam was _______ ibn Harithah.
Explain the transition from secret to public preaching and the Quraysh's reaction. [4 marks]
model_answer: For the first three years the Prophet preached secretly, holding meetings at Dar al-Arqam. First converts were Khadijah, Ali, Abu Bakr, and Zayd (RA). When Allah commanded 'warn your nearest relatives' (26:214), the Prophet gathered the Quraysh on Mount Safa and publicly proclaimed Islam. The Quraysh leaders rejected him and began persecuting Muslims because Islam threatened their idols, trade, and social status. His uncle Abu Lahab was among the most hostile opponents.

question: Cave Hira is situated on a mountain near Makkah called Jabal al-_______.
During the Hijrah, the Prophet and Abu Bakr (RA) hid in Cave _______ for three days.
Explain the significance of Cave Hira and Cave Thawr in the life of the Prophet (pbuh). [4 marks]
model_answer: Cave Hira on Jabal al-Nour was where the Prophet regularly retreated for prayer and contemplation before prophethood. It was here in 610 CE that Angel Jibreel revealed the first verses of Surah Al-Alaq, marking the beginning of prophethood. Cave Thawr is located south of Makkah where the Prophet and Abu Bakr hid for three days in 622 CE while escaping the Quraysh assassination plot during the Hijrah. Allah miraculously protected them — Hira represents the beginning of prophethood; Thawr the beginning of the Islamic state.

question: During the Isra, the Prophet travelled from Masjid al-Haram to Masjid al-_______ in Jerusalem.
The Prophet travelled on a heavenly creature called the _______ during the Isra.
During Mi'raj, Allah initially prescribed _______ daily prayers before reducing them to five.
The highest point the Prophet reached in the heavens is called Sidrat al-_______.
Describe the Isra and Mi'raj and explain their significance for the Muslim community. [4 marks]
model_answer: The Isra was the miraculous night journey from Makkah to Jerusalem on the Buraq, where the Prophet led all prophets in prayer. The Mi'raj was the ascension through the seven heavens where he met prophets including Adam, Musa, and Ibrahim. He reached Sidrat al-Muntaha and received the command for five daily prayers (reduced from fifty on Musa's advice). The significance: it honoured the Prophet after the Year of Grief, established Salah as an obligation, connected Islam to previous prophets, and demonstrated Allah's power and support.

question: The Prophet's companion during the visit to Ta'if was _______ ibn Harithah (RA).
The tribe whose leaders the Prophet met in Ta'if was Banu _______.
Describe the Prophet's visit to Ta'if and what it reveals about his character. [4 marks]
model_answer: After the Year of Grief (619 CE), the Prophet traveled to Ta'if with Zayd ibn Harithah seeking support. The three leaders of Banu Thaqif rejected and mocked him, setting slaves and children to stone them out of the city. Both were injured and bleeding. Despite being offered revenge by an angel, the Prophet refused and made a dua praying for their future guidance. This reveals his extraordinary patience, mercy, forgiveness, and complete trust in Allah — he prioritised the guidance of people over personal revenge.

question: The year in which both Khadijah (RA) and Abu Talib died is known as the Year of _______.
The people of _______ (later called Madinah) pledged to protect the Prophet in the Pledges of Aqabah.
Explain the main causes that led the Prophet (pbuh) to migrate from Makkah to Madinah. [4 marks]
model_answer: The main causes were: continuous Quraysh persecution including torture, economic boycott of Banu Hashim (616–619 CE), and social isolation. The Year of Grief (619 CE) saw the deaths of Khadijah (RA) and Abu Talib, leaving the Prophet without emotional and tribal protection. The mission to Ta'if failed completely. Most importantly, the Pledges of Aqabah (621–622 CE) provided a willing community in Yathrib ready to receive and protect him. The Quraysh then plotted to assassinate him, and Allah commanded the Hijrah.

question: Hazrat _______ (RA) slept in the Prophet's bed to deceive the Quraysh assassins on the night of Hijrah.
The first mosque built by the Prophet upon arriving near Madinah was Masjid _______.
The guide who led the Prophet and Abu Bakr safely to Madinah was Abdullah ibn _______.
Describe the main events that took place during the Hijrah journey. [4 marks]
model_answer: On the night of Hijrah, Ali (RA) slept in the Prophet's bed while he and Abu Bakr quietly escaped. They hid in Cave Thawr for three days; Asma (RA) brought food and news. They then traveled south via an unfamiliar route guided by Abdullah ibn Urayqit. Suraqah ibn Malik pursued them for a reward but his horse sank miraculously and he turned back. They arrived at Quba, built the first mosque, then entered Madinah on 12 Rabi al-Awwal 1 AH. The Ansar welcomed them joyfully. This Hijrah marks the start of the Islamic calendar.

question: Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA) was given the title '_______ ' for immediately believing in the Isra & Mi'raj.
Abu Bakr (RA) used his wealth to free the Muslim slave _______ (RA) from his tormentor.
Abu Bakr (RA) was the _______ Caliph of Islam after the Prophet's death.
Describe the contribution of Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA) to the early Muslim community. [4 marks]
model_answer: Abu Bakr (RA) was the first adult male convert and the Prophet's closest companion. He spent his wealth freeing tortured Muslims including Bilal (RA). He accompanied the Prophet during the Hijrah and hid with him in Cave Thawr. As First Caliph he united the Muslims after the Prophet's death, crushed the apostasy (Riddah) movements, and initiated the first written compilation of the Quran. He died after only two years as Caliph but left a lasting legacy of loyalty, sacrifice, and leadership.

question: Hazrat Usman (RA) was titled 'Dhul-Nurayn' because he married _______ daughters of the Prophet.
Usman (RA) was the _______ Caliph of Islam.
Usman (RA)'s greatest contribution was standardising the Quran into one official written copy called the _______.
Explain why the standardisation of the Quran under Uthman (RA) was important for the Muslim Ummah. [4 marks]
model_answer: As Islam spread to many regions, different dialect versions of the Quran caused disagreements over recitation. Uthman (RA) formed a committee led by Zayd ibn Thabit to produce one authoritative Mushaf in the Qurayshi dialect. Official copies were sent to all major cities and other versions burned to prevent division. This ensured the Quran remained unified and unchanged throughout the Muslim world. The Uthmanic Mushaf is the Quran Muslims use today — a lasting contribution that preserved Islamic unity.

question: Hazrat Ali (RA) was the _______ Caliph of Islam.
Hazrat Ali (RA) was known as 'Asadullah' meaning _______.
Hazrat Ali (RA) married _______ (RA), the youngest daughter of the Prophet.
Khadijah (RA) was _______ years old when she married the Prophet.
Khadijah (RA) was the _______ person to accept Islam.
Khadijah (RA) died in _______ CE, a year known as the Year of Grief.
Describe the role of Hazrat Khadijah (RA) in supporting the Prophet (pbuh) and spreading Islam. [4 marks]
model_answer: Khadijah (RA) was the first person to accept Islam and provided the Prophet with immediate emotional, financial, and moral support. When the first revelation came, she comforted him in his fear and took him to Waraqah ibn Nawfal for confirmation. She spent her entire fortune supporting early Muslims and the Prophet's mission. She was the mother of all his children except Ibrahim. Her death in 619 CE was a devastating loss for the Prophet. He honoured her memory throughout his life, sending gifts to her friends and speaking of her with deep love.

question: Hazrat Aisha (RA) narrated over _______ hadiths, making her one of the greatest narrators of hadith.
Hazrat Aisha (RA) was the daughter of _______ (RA), the First Caliph of Islam.
Explain the contribution of Hazrat Aisha (RA) to Islamic knowledge and scholarship. [4 marks]
model_answer: Hazrat Aisha (RA) was among the most learned companions, narrating over 2,200 hadiths. Her sharp memory and intelligence meant companions visited her to learn about the Prophet's daily life, worship, and rulings on family matters. Her knowledge extended to Islamic jurisprudence, Quranic commentary, medicine, and poetry. She taught men and women for over 40 years after the Prophet's death, becoming a cornerstone of Islamic scholarship. The Prophet himself declared: 'Take half of your religion from this red-cheeked woman.'

question: Hazrat Hafsa (RA) was the daughter of _______ ibn al-Khattab (RA).
The first official written Mushaf of the Quran was kept in the custody of Hazrat _______ (RA).
The first and most authoritative source of Islamic law is the _______.
The second primary source of Islamic law, recording the Prophet's sayings and actions, is the _______.
The science of deriving Islamic rulings from the Quran and Sunnah is called _______.
Explain the relationship between the Quran and Sunnah as the primary sources of Islamic law. [4 marks]
model_answer: The Quran is the direct word of Allah and provides the general principles and broad commands of Islamic law. The Sunnah, recorded in Hadith, is the Prophet's sayings, actions, and approvals, which explain and apply Quranic principles in practical situations. Together they form the foundation of Shariah — no ruling can contradict either. Where the Quran provides a general command (e.g. pray), the Sunnah provides the details (how, when, and how many times). Secondary sources like Ijma and Qiyas are only valid when aligned with these two primary sources.

question: Ijma refers to the _______ of qualified Muslim scholars on a religious ruling.
Ijma is the _______ source of Islamic law after the Quran and Sunnah.
Define Ijma and explain its importance as a source of Islamic law. [4 marks]
model_answer: Ijma is the unanimous consensus of qualified Muslim scholars on a religious ruling not explicitly stated in the Quran or Sunnah. Its authority is derived from the hadith 'My Ummah will never agree upon an error.' The strongest form is the Ijma of the Companions (Sahabah). Ijma allows Islamic law to remain relevant and address new situations that arise in every generation. Decisions reached through Ijma are binding on the Muslim community. It represents the collective wisdom of Islamic scholarship and prevents division over new religious questions.
